title: jasione crispa
intro: Jasione crispa, also known as Cat's Ear or Mountain Bluebells, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the bellflower family, Campanulaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving stress, and promoting relaxation. Additionally, Jasione crispa has been used to soothe skin irritations and wounds, making it a popular ingredient in natural skincare products.
